task: Deep Plan: System-Wide Standardization — Ecosystem Mapping
estimated_remaining_batches: 3-4 (Batch 3: Behavior/Rules [PRESENTED], Batch 4: Migration/Upgrade, Batch 5: CANON Spec Details, Batch 6: Edge Cases)

decisions.choice: `.canon/` at repo root — new top-level directory
decisions.rationale: Meta-system deserves top-level visibility, not buried under .planning/ or docs/
JSONL = canonical/AI-consumed, MD = human views generated from JSONL. Core tenet of the system moving forward.

decisions.choice: Hybrid: completeness tier checklist drives computed level (L0-L5)
decisions.rationale: Makes 'what's missing' obvious and progress measurable. Concrete capabilities compute to a level.

decisions.choice: Per-ecosystem files are source of truth + generated matrix view
decisions.rationale: Follows JSONL-first → generated-views pattern. Each ecosystem knows its own subsystems, matrix is computed.
Avoids 70+ hardcoded values while letting ecosystems specialize where needed.

decisions.choice: Dependency graph (CANON first) with maturity as tiebreaker (highest first)
decisions.rationale: Build momentum: early wins validate the framework before tackling hard stuff. CANON → near-compliant → major overhauls.

decisions.choice: Full ecosystem catalog + maturity assessments + sequenced plan + CANON spec
decisions.rationale: We have the research (500+ components). Comprehensive output avoids another discovery pass.

decisions.choice: Approved scale: L0=Nonexistent, L1=Identified, L2=Structured, L3=Monitored, L4=Enforced, L5=Canonized
decisions.rationale: Clear progression. L5 = all applicable subsystems canonized (not all 16 if some don't apply to ecosystem).

sub_decisions.l5_for_everything: L5 is the north star, not the exit criteria. Most ecosystems target L3-L4 baseline. L5 means all *applicable* subsystems canonized — per-ecosystem required-vs-optional mapping.
sub_decisions.plan_structure: Plan-as-you-go with sequenced placeholders. This deep plan produces catalog + sequence. Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when its turn comes. Pre-planning all 13 now would produce stale plans by ecosystem #8.

decisions.choice: 16-item checklist (original 13 + 3 gap-fills: inter-ecosystem contracts, rollback/recovery, deprecation policy)
decisions.rationale: User liked original 13, accepted 3 additions to close gaps.
checklist: 1. Zod schemas — for all data structures
2. JSONL storage — canonical data in JSONL format
3. Generated views — MD/JSON views computed from JSONL
4. Health monitoring — at least one health checker with metrics
5. Enforcement manifest — rules defining what's blocking vs warning
6. Testing — unit + integration tests for ecosystem scripts
8. State persistence — survives compaction / session boundaries
10. Naming compliance — follows CANON naming conventions
11. Configuration — uses central config (not hardcoded values)
12. Lifecycle hooks — creation, update, archival, deletion defined
14. Inter-ecosystem contracts — interfaces between dependent ecosystems
15. Rollback/recovery — revert to prior state after bad migration
16. Deprecation policy — how old schemas/APIs/patterns are sunset

decisions.choice: Pattern-level: CANON defines interface + reference pattern (PR Review v2 as exemplar)
decisions.rationale: Templates too rigid, interface-only too ambiguous. Reference patterns formalize what already exists.

decisions.choice: Primary ownership + consumer references (no superseding ecosystem)
decisions.rationale: Primary = maintenance responsibility, not authority hierarchy. Shared components have one owner, many consumers. CANON defines rules but doesn't own other ecosystems' components.

decisions.decision: CANON spec scope in this plan
decisions.choice: Working draft (structure + maturity model + subsystem interfaces + registry schema) with room for growth built in everywhere
decisions.rationale: Enough to start building CANON. Ecosystem assessments separate. Room for growth is a core tenet — nothing ships as a dead end.

sub_decisions.upgradeability: Core tenet: nothing not-upgradeable. Ease the upgrade process. Everything must have room for growth. Things change all the time.

decisions.choice: Semver (start at 0.1.0, promote to 1.0.0 after 2-3 ecosystem validations)
decisions.rationale: CANON is infrastructure others depend on. Breaking changes need explicit migration guidance.

questions: Q14: Enforcement model severity levels (A=4-tier, B=3-tier, C=5-tier, D=other). Recommended: A
Q15: Gate placement (A=pre-commit, B=pre-push, C=tiered, D=PR-only). Recommended: C
Q16: Naming convention depth (A=files, B=files+exports+JSONL, C=full taxonomy, D=minimal). Recommended: B
Q17: Health checker interface (A=as-is, B=+trend+recommendations, C=simpler, D=+dependency health). Recommended: B
Q18: Dashboard generation (A=on-demand, B=auto, C=hybrid, D=CI/CD only). Recommended: C
Q19: Migration strategy (A=big bang, B=incremental, C=layered, D=pilot+rollout). Recommended: D
Q20: Dependency declaration (A=per-ecosystem, B=centralized, C=both, D=implicit). Recommended: C

core_tenets.jsonl_first: JSONL is canonical and AI-consumed. MD is for human consumption, generated from JSONL info. Core tenet moving forward.
core_tenets.canon_is_ecosystem_zero: CANON is the meta-system above all others, lives at `.canon/` repo root
core_tenets.maturity_is_measurable: Completeness checklist (16 items) → computed level, not subjective grades
core_tenets.room_for_growth: Nothing ships as a dead end. Everything must be upgradeable. Ease the upgrade process. Things change all the time.
core_tenets.plan_as_you_go: This deep plan produces catalog + sequence. Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when its turn arrives. No stale pre-plans.

user_directives.roadmap_will_change: ROADMAP.md will need additions, changes, removals after this process
user_directives.grand_plan_reassessment: TDMS Grand Plan needs reassessment — changing many files invalidates existing debt items
user_directives.state_persistence_standard: Constant state file saving is a standard here AND repo-wide going forward — to be canonized as rules
user_directives.capture_all_ideas: Stream-of-consciousness ideas MUST be captured even if not immediately actionable
user_directives.canon_is_ecosystem_zero: CANON is the meta-system/Ecosystem Zero — building block for all others
user_directives.overlap_management: Many points of overlap between ecosystems — must not let one supersede another
user_directives.duplicate_subsystems: Cross-cutting subsystems exist in multiple ecosystems and need mapping
user_directives.standardization_is_key: Duplicate subsystems must follow shared standards
user_directives.pr_creep_guard: User has tendency toward 30-50 commit PRs. Wants guardrail mechanism SOON.
user_directives.lifecycle_management: User agreed — helps future-proof. Added to cross-cutting subsystems.
user_directives.jsonl_is_canon: JSONL is canonical/AI-consumed, MD is human-readable generated from JSONL. Core tenet.
user_directives.room_for_growth_everywhere: Nothing not-upgradeable. Ease upgrade process. Room for growth built in everywhere. Things change all the time.
user_directives.no_superseding_ecosystem: Primary ownership = maintenance responsibility, not authority. No ecosystem supersedes another.
user_directives.plan_as_you_go: Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when sequenced. Don't pre-plan all 13 — they'd go stale.
user_directives.ecosystem_plan_sequencing_matters: Dependency-wise sequencing between ecosystem plans is important. Discoveries in one plan may feed into another.
user_directives.mcp_memory_enabled: enableAllProjectMcpServers flipped to true. Verify memory MCP tools available next session.

ideas_captured: ROADMAP entries will change significantly post-standardization
Grand Plan debt items may become invalid as files change
State persistence should use whatever resources are best
Cross-ecosystem subsystem mapping is its own discovery task
Canon is Ecosystem Zero — all others derive from it
Planning standardization (deep-plan, GSD) as backbone — build from there
PR creep guardrail: commit counter hook (warn 10, block 25, override available)
Branch scope declaration idea: S/M/L with commit brackets
Session-end PR gate: flag if >15 commits not in a PR
State saving rules need to be canonized
Configuration as single source of truth vs hardcoded values
L5 is north star not exit criteria — most target L3-L4 baseline
Upgrade-friendliness as design principle in every component
MCP memory as secondary state backup (verify next session)
Configure episodic memory for remote sessions (broader benefit beyond this plan)
resume_instructions: Phase 0 APPROVED. Batch 1 COMPLETE (7 decisions). Batch 2 COMPLETE (6 decisions, total 13). Batch 3 PRESENTED (7 questions Q14-Q20) but NOT YET ANSWERED. MCP memory setting changed (enableAllProjectMcpServers=true) — verify tools available. Next: collect Batch 3 answers, save state, present Batch 4.
